prompt:
Use case: stylized-concept
Asset type: battle result screen art panel for a science-fantasy tactics RPG
Primary request: create a victory illustration for a mission-complete overlay
Scene/background: a reclaimed chapel ridge after battle, with battered stone stairs leading toward a secured altar, rekindled braziers, torn but upright war banners, and distant mountain haze under the last warm light
Subject: the battlefield should feel solemn, hard-won, martial, and sacred rather than celebratory or cheerful
Style/medium: painterly pixel-art-inspired 2D environment matte, matching existing Renations backdrops and terrain art more than standalone concept art, late-90s / early-2000s strategy RPG tone
Composition/framing: wide horizontal composition designed for a result panel, strong readable foreground stone and banner shapes, clear midground altar focal point, open negative space for UI text on one side, no characters
Lighting/mood: ember-lit dusk victory, restrained warm light on worn stone, tragic calm after the fighting, reverent and disciplined rather than triumphant spectacle
Color palette: parchment ivory, brass, warm chapel gold, worn stone gray, moss green, dark wine, ember orange, ash violet in the distance
Materials/textures: cracked flagstones, weathered cloth, carved stone, lingering smoke haze, soft cloud bands
Quality: high
Constraints: no characters, no text, no UI, no modern objects, no glossy 3D, no anime key art, no confetti, no oversized empty sky, no busy tiny detail noise; keep it readable when partially covered by interface copy
Avoid: photorealism, mobile fantasy splash art, heroic poster posing, neon magic, cartoon exaggeration, cluttered rubble everywhere

Use case: stylized-concept
Asset type: battle result screen art panel for a science-fantasy tactics RPG
Primary request: create a defeat illustration for a mission-failed overlay
Scene/background: a desecrated chapel ridge under smoke-heavy twilight, with a shadowed altar, extinguished braziers, broken standards dragged across cracked flagstones, and cold wind over the valley beyond
Subject: the battlefield should feel grim, solemn, and costly, with the sense that the line has broken and the enemy now owns the ground
Style/medium: painterly pixel-art-inspired 2D environment matte, consistent with existing Renations battle backdrops and terrain art, late-90s / early-2000s strategy RPG mood rather than cinematic poster art
Composition/framing: wide horizontal composition intended for a result panel, strong silhouette shapes, altar and ruined ridge as the main read, room for UI text, no characters
Lighting/mood: ash-dark dusk with sparse ember remnants, cold stone, muted wine-red cloth, tragic ecclesiastical atmosphere, oppressive but still grounded and readable
Color palette: worn stone gray, ash brown, dark wine, ember cinder orange, muted chapel gold, soot black, pale smoke teal only in distant haze
Materials/textures: shattered masonry, soot-stained cloth, broken ironwork, drifting ash, rough cliff stone, smoke layers
Quality: high
Constraints: no characters, no text, no UI, no photobash look, no glossy 3D, no anime glamour, no giant monsters, no bright magical spectacle; keep the image readable at a glance behind overlay text
Avoid: photorealism, saturated red horror lighting, posterized contrast, cluttered debris noise, generic dark fantasy wallpaper
